results.hypothesis: Agent tool's mode parameter is honored at spawn time
results.deviation_from_protocol: Used subagent_type=builder instead of state-manager because state-manager's tool list does not include Write — mode=acceptEdits applies to edit tools (Write/Edit/NotebookEdit), so the test requires an agent that actually has those tools.
results.evidence: Teammate reported 'Write tool executed immediately with no permission prompt. Created /tmp/mode-test.txt with content hello on first call with no interruption, denial, or approval gate.' File existence confirmed by host.
results.conclusion: mode parameter IS honored at spawn time. Spawning a teammate with mode='acceptEdits' makes Write/Edit tools auto-approve without prompts.

results.hypothesis: When spawn prompt requires plan approval, teammate sends a structured plan_approval_request message
results.evidence: Teammate sent plain markdown text titled 'Plan approval request:' rather than a structured plan_approval_request JSON. The teammate did not call ExitPlanMode — it just composed a text message and asked for approval.
results.conclusion: When plan approval is requested only via spawn prompt instructions (without mode='plan'), the teammate respects the gate but uses PLAIN TEXT rather than a structured plan_approval_request. The structured protocol requires ExitPlanMode, which is only triggered automatically when the teammate is actually in plan mode (see Test 3).

results.hypothesis: mode='plan' makes teammate start in read-only plan mode and follow ExitPlanMode → plan_approval_request flow
results.started_in_plan_mode_evidence: Harness injected a system-reminder stating 'Plan mode is active' with a designated plan file path (/Users/ether/.claude/plans/precious-riding-castle.md) and restricted edits to that file only.
results.write_blocked_evidence: Plan-mode system reminder explicitly forbade non-readonly tools. Only the designated plan file at the harness path was writable during plan mode.
results.plan_approval_request_evidence: Received structured JSON with type='plan_approval_request', from='test-plan-mode', planFilePath, planContent, and requestId='plan_approval-1776136680699@test-plan-mode@api-test'. Triggered by the teammate's ExitPlanMode call.
results.approval_method_that_worked: Structured plan_approval_response message via SendMessage with matching request_id and approve=true
results.post_approval_evidence: Teammate reported: 'After your approval arrived, permissionMode was bypassPermissions (per the approval response metadata). Write to /tmp/plan-mode-test.txt succeeded immediately on first try.' Host confirmed file exists with content 'test'.
results.conclusion: mode='plan' fully wires the structured plan-approval flow: teammate starts in plan mode, Write/Edit blocked by harness, ExitPlanMode triggers a structured plan_approval_request to the lead, plan_approval_response with approve=true unblocks the teammate AND auto-transitions its permission mode to bypassPermissions for the post-approval execution.

additional_findings: Plan approval flow REQUIRES mode='plan' to get the structured protocol. Spawn-prompt-only plan-approval instructions produce plain text plans (Test 2), while mode='plan' produces structured plan_approval_request messages (Test 3).

ExitPlanMode is the harness mechanism that converts a teammate's plan into a structured plan_approval_request — it does not unilaterally exit plan mode; it submits the plan and holds the teammate in a waiting state until the lead responds.

After plan_approval_response with approve=true, the teammate's permission mode auto-transitions to bypassPermissions for the post-approval execution phase. Subsequent Write/Edit calls succeed immediately without further prompts.
state-manager subagent does NOT have Write in its tools list — any test that requires Write must use builder or another agent type with edit tools.
implications_for_agent_atelier: The Architect/Orchestrator should rely on mode='plan' (not just prompt instructions) when spawning Builders for complex work items, to get the structured plan_approval_request flow that the orchestrator can detect programmatically.

The current Plan Review Protocol in plugins/agent-atelier/references/prompts/orchestrator.md should distinguish between 'plain text plan messages' (informal review) and 'plan_approval_request structured messages' (structured review with request_id tracking).
Setting mode='acceptEdits' at spawn time is a reliable way to give Builders auto-approve permissions without triggering interactive prompts — useful for trusted automation paths.
